name: Jwt

unions.name: JwtError
unions.comment:
The following errors are modeled

  - 401 (Unauthorized), due either to token expiry or e.g. inadequate permissions
  - token (non-)expiry information
  - issues with processing (e.g. base 64 decoding) the token
  - problems decoding the json data within the content of the token
  - Any Http.Error, other than a 401

values.name: checkTokenExpiry
values.comment: Checks a token for Expiry. Returns expiry or any errors that occurred in decoding.

values.name: createRequest
values.comment:
createRequest creates a Http.Request with the token added to the headers, and
sets the `withCredentials` field to True.
values.type: String.String -> String.String -> String.String -> Http.Body -> Json.Decode.Decoder a -> Http.Request a

values.name: createRequestObject
values.comment:
createRequestObject creates the data structure expected by Http.Request.
It is broken out here so that users can change the expect part in the event that
one of their REST apis does not return Json.

In my experience, the Authorization header is NOT case sensitive. Do raise an issue if you experience otherwise.

See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/XMLHttpRequest/withCredentials) for more on withCredentials. The default is False.
values.type: String.String -> String.String -> String.String -> Http.Body -> Json.Decode.Decoder a -> { method : String.String, headers : List.List Http.Header, url : String.String, body : Http.Body, expect : Http.Expect a, timeout : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Float, withCredentials : Basics.Bool }

values.name: decodeToken
values.comment:
decodeToken parses the token, checking that it meets the Jwt standards.

    decodeToken dec token

In the event of success, `decodeToken` returns an Elm record structure using the JSON Decoder.
values.type: Json.Decode.Decoder a -> String.String -> Result.Result Jwt.JwtError a

values.name: delete
values.comment: returns a `DELETE` Http.Request with the token attached to the headers.
values.type: String.String -> String.String -> Json.Decode.Decoder a -> Http.Request a

values.name: get
values.comment:
`get` is a replacement for `Http.get` that returns a Http.Request with the token
attached to the headers.

    getData : String -> Cmd Msg
    getData token =
        Jwt.get token "/api/data" dataDecoder
            |> Jwt.send DataResult
values.type: String.String -> String.String -> Json.Decode.Decoder a -> Http.Request a

values.name: handleError
values.comment: Takes an Http.Error. If it is a 401, check the token for expiry.

values.name: isExpired
values.comment:
Checks whether a token has expired, and returns True or False, or
any error that occurred while decoding the token.

values.name: post
values.comment:
post is a replacement for `Http.post` that returns a Http.Request with the token
attached to the headers.

** Note that is important to use jsonBody to ensure that the 'application/json' is added to the headers **

    postContent : Token -> Decoder a -> E.Value -> String -> Request a
    postContent token dec value url =
        Jwt.post token url (Http.jsonBody value) (phoenixDecoder dec)
            |> Jwt.send ContentResult
values.type: String.String -> String.String -> Http.Body -> Json.Decode.Decoder a -> Http.Request a

values.name: promote401
values.comment:
Examines a 401 Unauthorized reponse, and converts the error to TokenExpired
when that is the case.

    getAuth : String -> String -> Decoder a -> Task Never (Result JwtError a)
    getAuth token url dec =
        createRequest "GET" token url Http.emptyBody dec
            |> toTask
            |> Task.map Result.Ok
            |> Task.onError (promote401 token)

values.name: put
values.comment: Create a PUT request with a token attached to the Authorization header
values.type: String.String -> String.String -> Http.Body -> Json.Decode.Decoder a -> Http.Request a

values.name: send
values.comment: `send` replaces `Http.send`. On receipt of a 401 error, it returns a Jwt.Unauthorized.
values.type: (Result.Result Jwt.JwtError a -> msg) -> Http.Request a -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg

values.name: sendCheckExpired
values.comment:
`sendCheckExpired` is similar to `send` but, on receiving a 401, it carries out a further check to
determine whether the token has expired.
values.type: String.String -> (Result.Result Jwt.JwtError a -> msg) -> Http.Request a -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg

values.name: tokenDecoder
values.comment:
All the token parsing goodness in the form of a Json Decoder

    -- decode token from Firebase
    let firebaseToken =
            decodeString (tokenDecoder Jwt.Decoders.firebase) tokenString

name: Jwt.Decoders
comment:
Helper functions for working with Jwt tokens and authenticated CRUD APIs.

This package provides functions for reading tokens, and for using them to make
authenticated Http requests.


# Decoders for popular Jwt tokens

@docs JwtToken, firebase, phoenixGuardian

aliases.name: JwtToken
aliases.comment: Generic constructor for commonly found fields in a Jwt token
aliases.type: { iat : Basics.Int, exp : Basics.Int, userId : Maybe.Maybe String.String, email : Maybe.Maybe String.String }
